finding: Pulmonary metastases occur in 30% of all malignancies; mostly hematogenous
context: Age:   > 50 years (in 87%) Incidence of pulmonary metastases: Common primaries of intravascular metastases: breast, stomach, liver, kidney, lung, prostate, choriocarcinoma ◊  Right atrial myxoma + RCC tend to embolize to large central + segmental pulmonary arteries √  multiple nodules (in 75%), 82% subpleural: √  often smooth + well-defined √  varying sizes (most typical) √  usually in random distribution √  fine micronodular pattern: highly vascular tumor (renal cell, breast, thyroid, prostate carcinoma, bone sarcoma, choriocarcinoma) √  pneumothorax (2%): especially in children with sarcoma
